_meta.description: Full brand discovery interview question bank. Used by brand-consultant agent when /brand command is invoked. Questions flow in 7 phases. Each question has a type, choices (if applicable), and maps to an output field.

phases.name: Brand Foundation
phases.description: Understanding what your brand is and where it stands today.

questions.question: What is your brand's primary offering?
Where is your brand in its journey?
choices: A — Just starting out (pre-launch or < 1 year)
B — Established but rebranding or repositioning
C — Growing and formalising our voice
D — Mature brand looking to evolve our content
E — Launching a new product or service line

questions.question: What geographic market does your brand primarily serve?
choices: A — Local city (one city or area)
C — Regional (e.g. Middle East, North Africa, GCC)

questions.question:
In one sentence — what does your brand do, for whom, and why it matters?
(Type your answer freely)
questions.placeholder: e.g. We design bespoke residential interiors for high-net-worth clients in Cairo who want spaces that outlast trends.

phases.name: Your Audience
phases.description: Who you are speaking to shapes everything about how you speak.

questions.question: Who is your primary client or customer?
choices: C — Developers and real estate investors
D — Young professionals and aspirational buyers
E — Families investing in their primary home
G — Both B2B and B2C equally

questions.question: What does your ideal client care most about? (Choose up to 3)
choices: J — Price and value for money

questions.question: What is the one thing your client fears or wants to avoid most?
choices: A — Wasting money on poor quality
B — Getting something generic or trend-driven
C — Losing control over the outcome
D — The process being stressful or unclear
E — A result that doesn't feel like them
F — Being overcharged or poorly treated

questions.question:
Describe your ideal client in 2–3 sentences. Who are they really?
(Type your answer freely)
questions.placeholder: e.g. She's 45, runs her own business in Zamalek, has travelled extensively and is tired of Egyptian design that tries to look European. She wants something singular.

phases.name: Brand Personality
phases.description: If your brand were a person at a dinner table, how would they show up?

questions.question: Which of these personalities is closest to your brand?
choices: A — The Expert — authoritative, precise, commands respect through depth
B — The Creator — imaginative, expressive, pushes boundaries
C — The Guide — warm, helpful, empowers the client's own vision
D — The Rebel — challenges conventions, distinctive, unapologetically different
E — The Sage — philosophical, considered, shares knowledge generously
F — The Ruler — premium, established, confident in its authority
G — The Companion — friendly, approachable, genuinely relatable
H — The Explorer — curious, adventurous, drawn to discovery

questions.question: Choose 3 words that should define your brand's voice.
Where does your brand sit on each spectrum? (Answer A through E for each)

phases.name: Positioning & Differentiation
phases.description: What makes your brand impossible to substitute.

questions.question: What is your price positioning?
choices: A — Ultra-premium (top 1–5%, bespoke, no published prices)
B — Premium (high-end, published price ranges, selective)
C — Mid-market premium (quality at accessible price point)
D — Value-focused (best quality for the price)

questions.question:
What do you genuinely do that your competitors don't or can't?
(Type freely — be specific. This is your most important answer.)
questions.placeholder: e.g. We source all natural materials directly from artisans in Morocco and Italy — no middlemen. We also limit ourselves to 6 commissions per year to ensure depth of attention.

questions.question: What do you deliberately NOT do? (What separates you by what you refuse?)
questions.placeholder: e.g. We never take trend-driven briefs. We don't rush. We don't offer 3D renders as a substitute for real material samples.

questions.question: Name 2–3 competitors or brands in your space (don't worry — we use this to differentiate, not copy).
questions.placeholder: e.g. Studio X in Cairo, Zaha Hadid Architects (aspirational), [local competitor name]

phases.name: Voice & Language
phases.description: The specific words, rhythms, and rules that make your writing unmistakably yours.

questions.question: How should your content make the reader feel? (Choose up to 2)
choices: H — Calm and confident in their choice

questions.question:
What language or phrases should NEVER appear in your content?
(List words, phrases, or types of language to ban.)
questions.placeholder: e.g. 'Amazing', 'stunning', 'world-class', 'game-changing', 'passionate about', 'luxury' overused, exclamation marks, listicles

questions.question: What words or phrases feel most like YOUR brand? (Ones you naturally use.)
How should your calls to action feel?
choices: A — Invitation: soft, no pressure ('When you're ready, we're here')
B — Confidence: clear and direct ('Begin a conversation →')
C — Curiosity: draws them in ('See how we approach this →')
D — Action: clear next step ('Book a consultation')
E — Authority: positions us as the natural choice ('Work with the studio →')

questions.question: What language rules matter to you? (Choose all that apply)
choices: A — British English spelling (colour, realise, centre)
B — American English spelling (color, realize, center)
C — Oxford comma always (x, y, and z)
D — No exclamation marks in editorial content
F — No listicles — prose only for blog content
G — Numbers written out (one through nine)
H — Em dash (—) with spaces around it

phases.name: Content Strategy
phases.description: What you publish, how often, and why.

questions.question: What are the main topics you want your brand to own? (Choose up to 4)
choices: C — Sustainability and ethics in design
D — Client stories and case studies
E — Trend analysis and market insight
G — Education: how to commission design
H — Cultural commentary on design and space
I — Investment value of good design
J — Local design scene and scene-setting

questions.question: Which content formats do you want to prioritise?
choices: D — Website core pages (About, Services, etc.)
H — Press releases and media pitches

questions.question: What primary SEO goal are you focused on?
choices: A — Local search (finding us by city + service)
B — Authority content (ranking for industry topics)
D — Direct competitor displacement (outranking specific studios)

phases.name: Reference & Inspiration
phases.description: Showing us what resonates tells us more than describing it.

questions.question: Name 2–3 brands (any industry) whose communication style you admire and why.
questions.placeholder: e.g. Aesop — precise, literary, never salesy. Kinfolk magazine — warm but considered. Bottega Veneta — says a lot by saying little.

questions.question: Name 1–2 brands whose communication style you absolutely do NOT want to emulate.
questions.placeholder: e.g. Generic real estate marketing (all caps, hype), mass-market design influencers (trend-chasing, shallow)

questions.question:
Paste a paragraph of your own existing content that you're proud of (or that feels most 'you').
If you don't have any, skip this.
questions.placeholder: Paste any text from your website, a past email, a proposal, or social caption that feels right.

questions.question: What does success look like for your brand content in 12 months?
choices: A — More qualified enquiries from our ideal client
B — Recognition as a thought leader in our space
C — Better search visibility and organic traffic
D — A cohesive, professional brand presence across all channels
E — A content library we can consistently publish from
F — Reduced reliance on referrals — inbound leads

questions.question:
Is there anything else about your brand, your values, or what you do that you think is essential for anyone writing content for you to know?
(This is your open mic — use it.)
questions.placeholder: Any cultural context, personal philosophy, non-negotiables, or important backstory that informs your brand.
